subject: Human Resources
title: Independent Contractors vs. Employees
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Independent Contractors vs. Employees.

hook.text: A rideshare company classifies 3 million drivers as contractors—avoiding benefits, overtime, and minimum wage.

buildup.text: The distinction matters: workers get legal protections, benefits, and tax withholding.

discovery.text: The IRS uses a multi-factor test—examining behavioral control, financial control, and relationship type—for classification.

climax.text: California's AB5 and EU directives are tightening classification—pushing companies to provide more gig worker protections.

punchline.text: Calling someone a contractor doesn't make them one. The law looks at reality, not labels.

quiz.question: What factors does the IRS examine for worker classification?
options: Behavioral control, financial control, and relationship type
Whether the worker wants to be a contractor
